name: Shoulder MRI
description: MRI of the shoulder for evaluation of rotator cuff, labrum, and shoulder joint pathology

findings.technique: MRI of the shoulder was performed in multiple planes including T1, T2, PD, and STIR sequences.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Rotator Cuff
sections.content: The supraspinatus tendon demonstrates [no tear / no thinning / no discontinuity]. The infraspinatus and teres minor tendons are [intact / normal].

sections.name: Rotator Cuff Muscles
sections.content: The supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and subscapularis muscles demonstrate [no atrophy / no fatty infiltration / normal bulk].

sections.name: Labrum
sections.content: The glenoid labrum demonstrates [no tear / no Bankart lesion / no SLAP lesion]. The labral attachment is [intact / normal].

sections.name: Biceps Tendon
sections.content: The biceps tendon is [intracapsular / no subluxation / no dislocation / no tear]. The biceps anchor is [intact / normal].

sections.name: Glenohumeral Ligaments
sections.content: The inferior glenohumeral ligament complex is [intact / no thickening / no tear]. The middle and superior glenohumeral ligaments are [normal].

sections.name: Acromioclavicular Joint
sections.content: The AC joint demonstrates [no osteoarthritis / no inferior spurring / no separation]. The distal clavicle is [intact].

sections.name: Bone
sections.content: The humeral head demonstrates [no Hill-Sachs lesion / no deformity]. The glenoid is [intact / no fracture]. No bone bruise.

sections.name: Joint Effusion
sections.content: There is [minimal / no / small] joint effusion. No loose body identified.

impression:
1. Normal shoulder MRI.
2. No rotator cuff tear, labral tear, or instability finding identified.
author: RadReport Templates
